title: Living in Boulder vlog 10 - 30 - 2017: Got to battles 1575 linking to Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief health update about a staph infection from an old cancer removal site, then talks about his ongoing project linking battle entries to a historical timeline, currently at 1575. He shares a prediction for finishing through the 1800s by Wednesday and mentions a YouTube channel called Historia Civilis and an idea he has for Google Earth battle visualization videos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Not too worried since it's not in the same spot as the previous cancer
Making steady progress and expects to finish connecting flickr/Google files through the 1900s by end of Wednesday
Thinks it's really good for explaining battles with flash animation
Wants to make videos showing battle distances from a participant's perspective

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to the dermatologist because I thought I had cancer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dermatologist confirmed it wasn't cancer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I developed a staph infection after the procedure.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't take the antibiotics correctly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dermatologist said I shouldn't worry because the infection isn't in the same place as the previous cancer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on connecting battle lengths to my timeline and files.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I was at around 900 in my work progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I'm at 1575 in my work progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow I plan to work through the 1610s, 1620s, and 1630s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I aim to catch up to the 1800s by tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to create YouTube videos about battles using Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to show the distances people covered in battles from a runner's perspective.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I watched a YouTube channel called Historia Civilis that explains battles with flash animations.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Historia Civilis covers Roman and political history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the Historia Civilis videos are really good.

transcript.text: Hello Living in Boulder Vlog, 4.41am, October 31st, 2017, so today, I think I got up kind

Was working a little bit, and then I went into the dermatologist, because I got a referral
For that, I thought that it was cancer, because I got cancer here, like a year and a half
Ago, and came back, nothing, it took it out totally, but then it came back like it was
With cancer, so I was happy about that, but then this turned into a staph infection, I
Think a little stitch got stuck in it, and so they referred me to the dermatologist, they
Got me on antibiotics, but then I got a refill, because I think I didn't take it right, not
As much as I should have, but he said I shouldn't worry about it, because it's not the
Same place as where, hmm, I had the cancer before, I was like an inch away, so, yeah,
That's what I did today, then I came back, and, um, was working, yeah, so I'm working on
The battles, connecting battle links to my timeline, to my Google Earth files, and, uh, I think
Last time I did the video, I think it was yesterday, I was at, um, um, like the 900s or something
Like that, so today, right now, I'm at, like, 1575, so, tomorrow, yeah, it's three more decades,
16, 17s, and 18s, and then I get caught up with what I'm doing, with the, uh, creating
The files, connecting to, uh, uh, Flickr to the Google Earth file, so, um, before I
Save it as the file that Google, uh, you know, Dreamweaver connects to, what is that, so, um,
Yeah, yeah, see how many more days, I mean, you know, I only do a few hundreds a day, uh,
Okay, yeah, yeah, so let's do the predictions, so tomorrow, I say, I gotta do one quarter of
The 15s, I say I get through the 15s, and then I do all the 16s and 17s, I bet you I can
Get shit, get to the beginning of 1800 tomorrow, and then it's just the 1800s, which I can do
In one day, so, yeah, two more days, so, Tuesday and Wednesday, by the end of Wednesday, we're
Done, oh, wow, I'll be caught up, and then I can finish the, um, 1900s with, uh, you know,
Flickr, so, um, what was I listening to, oh, I was listening to this really great video,
I mean, YouTube channel called, uh, History Civitas, yeah, I think that's what it's called,
History, well, it's, we'll check exactly, Historia Civilis, Historia Civilis, explaining
Battles with, uh, Flash, the whole mechanics of it, and it's really good, and then also
Other, you know, Roman stuff, flip political stuff, but, um, yeah, I think I want to make
YouTube videos of battles with, uh, Google Earth, and, uh, like from a, like a bicycle
Runner's perspective, where you show the distances that all the people in the battle did, you
Know, because, uh, because they were running from all of that, um, yeah, okay, so, that's
